figure:
Drill Holes:
(0010) Jackhammer, 2.5-inch diameter (medium)
(0020) Jackhammer, 2.5-inch diameter (hard)
(0030) Crawler-mounted rock drill, 2.5-inch diameter (medium)
(0040) Crawler-mounted rock drill, 2.5-inch diameter (hard)
(0050) Load and shoot holes
Minimum suggested crew size: Jackhammer—one operator per drill\nCrawler-mounted rock drill—two operators per drill\nLoad/shoot—minimum of four crew members.
Note: Times may vary depending on type of rock, equipment, and/or explosives.

Drill Holes:
(0010) Jackhammer, 2.5-inch diameter (medium)
(0020) Jackhammer, 2.5-inch diameter (hard)
(0030) Crawler-mounted rock drill, 2.5-inch diameter (medium)
(0040) Crawler-mounted rock drill, 2.5-inch diameter (hard)
(0050) Load and shoot holes
Minimum suggested crew size: Jackhammer—one operator per drill\nCrawler-mounted rock drill—two operators per drill\nLoad/shoot—minimum of four crew members.
Note: Times may vary depending on type of rock, equipment, and/or explosives.

(0010) Place jute mesh, plastic netting, or polypropylene membrane
(0030) Place nylon, 3 dimensional geomatrix, 12 mil thick
(0110) Machine-place riprap materiel
(0120) Hand-fill voids in riprap
Minimum suggested crew size: Place riprap—two operators for crane, using tongs/clamshell, with two\ncrewmembers on tag lines placing rock.
Note: Placing riprap should entail 120 man-hours if interlocked.
